themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner has become a dominant transnational story, with right-wing and far-right media across multiple countries amplifying the incident as a symbol of rising political violence. Leaders like Giorgia Meloni and Marine Le Pen have issued statements condemning the attack, framing it as an assault on democratic values.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Extensive coverage of the shooting, Trump’s evacuation, and global reactions, with emphasis on political violence and security failures.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemned the attack, linking it to broader concerns about political hatred and democratic erosion.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella issued statements condemning the attack, framing it as part of a global trend of political extremism.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage and Reform UK highlighted the incident as evidence of growing instability in Western democracies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox framed the attack as a consequence of left-wing rhetoric, calling for stronger security measures for political leaders.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, with the AfD in Germany and Vox in Spain leading the charge. This trend reflects growing voter dissatisfaction with mainstream parties, immigration policies, and economic instability. The coverage highlights parallel electoral successes and the normalization of far-right rhetoric in political discourse.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD reaches record highs in polls, surpassing the CDU/CSU in some surveys, with intense focus on immigration and economic grievances.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox gains momentum in regional elections, particularly in Andalusia, with a focus on anti-immigration policies and national sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella lead in polls for the 2027 presidential election, capitalizing on economic and immigration concerns.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD continues to gain influence in Swedish politics, pushing for stricter immigration policies and collaboration with mainstream right-wing parties.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP consolidates its position as a key player in Norwegian politics, advocating for reduced immigration and tax cuts.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalition governments between Spain’s Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regions like Extremadura and Aragón has ignited a transnational debate over the legality and implications of their 'national priority' policies. These policies, which prioritize Spanish citizens in access to social services, have drawn comparisons to far-right policies in other European countries and sparked legal challenges from opposition parties.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox pushes 'national priority' policies in coalition agreements with PP, sparking legal and political backlash.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP defends its coalition with Vox, arguing that the policies are legal and necessary to address voter concerns.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 PSOE
countries.note: PSOE condemns the PP-Vox pacts as unconstitutional and discriminatory, vowing to challenge them in court.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN draws parallels between Vox’s policies and its own proposals, framing them as part of a broader European shift toward nationalism.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian far-right media highlight the PP-Vox pacts as evidence of a growing trend toward nationalist governance in Europe.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to step down from Hungary’s parliament following his party’s electoral defeat has sent shockwaves through the country’s political landscape. The rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party has prompted a broader discussion about the future of right-wing populism in Central Europe, with parallels drawn to other countries where far-right leaders have faced electoral setbacks.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán’s resignation and the rise of Tisza dominate coverage, with debates over the future of Fidesz and Hungary’s political direction.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media draw parallels between Orbán’s resignation and PiS’s internal struggles, framing it as a cautionary tale for right-wing parties.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD media highlight Orbán’s resignation as a sign of the vulnerabilities of right-wing populist leaders in Europe.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU-focused media analyze Orbán’s resignation in the context of Hungary’s strained relationship with Brussels, particularly over rule-of-law concerns.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Incidents of political violence linked to far-right rhetoric have surged across Europe and the U.S., with media coverage highlighting attacks on political opponents, journalists, and minority groups. The theme underscores the growing normalization of violent discourse in right-wing and far-right circles, with parallels drawn between incidents in Italy, Spain, and the U.S.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Coverage of violent incidents during Italy’s Liberation Day celebrations, including attacks on protesters and vandalism of party offices.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s rhetoric is linked to attacks on party members and stalls during public events, with accusations of inciting violence.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK faces criticism for its role in fueling anti-immigrant sentiment, with reports of harassment and threats against political opponents.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric is linked to rising political violence, including the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting and other incidents.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN’s discourse is scrutinized for its role in escalating tensions, particularly during protests and public rallies.
